mapping.description:
Parses CSV files produced by ORC that enumerate running processes. It extracts each process’s metadata, such as name, executable path, timestamps, identifiers, and resource usage.
- Process name, command‑line and executable location. - Creation (`creation`) and termination (`termination`) timestamps. - Parent/child relationship via `parent_id` and `process_id`. - Resource metrics: memory size, CPU times, priority, session. - Execution context: user‑mode/kernel‑mode time, responding state, privileged flag.

field.description: executable name of the process (without path)
total number of handles opened by the process
full filesystem path to the process executable
publisher name extracted from the executable's version info
cumulative CPU time consumed by the process
file version string from the executable's version resource
product version string from the executable's version resource
product name extracted from the executable's version info
title of the process's main window, if present
human‑readable description from the executable's version info
timestamp when the process was created
timestamp when the process terminated (if applicable)
CPU time spent in kernel‑mode (privileged) for the process
CPU time spent in user‑mode for the process
total CPU time (user + kernel) consumed by the process
process identifier (PID) of the parent process
string representation of the process's priority class
numeric priority value of the process
process identifier (PID) of the current process
identifier of the session in which the process runs
indicates whether the process is currently responding (True/False)
virtual memory size allocated to the process (in bytes)
